response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a full-stack TypeScript/Node.js engineer with roughly 15 years of experience, but his profile does not meet the core mandatory requirements for this role. C++ is confined to approximately 6 months at National Instruments (2014–2015) writing components for an IDE, plus a bachelor thesis project — far short of the required 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. There is no meaningful SQL Server or Oracle experience, no Windows platform work, and no capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain exposure whatsoever. The financial-domain stint at RIDE Capital was Node/TypeScript/GraphQL work, not C++. The optional desirable skills (CI/CD) partially apply, but they do not offset the critical gaps in the mandatory criteria.
strengths: Strong CI/CD and DevOps background (GitLab CI, Docker, trunk-based development) satisfies one desirable criterion
Demonstrated ability to work in high-throughput, low-latency systems (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby, millions of HTTP req/s) shows systems-level thinking
Polyglot developer with Java, DLang, and some C/C++ exposure, indicating capacity to work in compiled, statically-typed languages
concerns: C++ experience totals roughly 6 months (NI, 2014–2015) — critically below the 5–10 year mandatory requirement
Zero capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain knowledge; financial-sector role at RIDE Capital used no relevant tech stack
No Windows platform development history and no SQL Server/Oracle experience evident in the resume
